description_long: This is a classical-style bust sculpture depicting the head and upper neck of a bearded man with deep-set eyes, pronounced brow, and detailed curly hair and beard. The facial expression is stern and contemplative, with the mouth slightly open and the beard flowing down to the base. The back of the head features intricate hair texture, though there is significant damage or erosion on one side, exposing a rough, hollow interior. The sculpture is mounted on a simple, rounded pedestal base. The surface appears to be made of white or light beige marble, with a matte, slightly rough finish. The overall form and craftsmanship suggest it is modeled after an ancient philosopher or notable historical figure.
description: A marble bust of a bearded man with detailed facial features and hair, mounted on a rounded pedestal. The sculpture shows significant damage on the back, revealing a hollow interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed face, beard, and expressive features of the bust.
description_view_1: Rear three-quarter view highlighting the textured hair and the undamaged side of the head.
description_view_2: Direct rear view revealing a large damaged area with a hollow interior and rough edges.
description_view_3: Side profile view displaying the full beard, ear, and the curvature of the head and neck.
attribute_subject_matter: bearded male human figure, possibly a philosopher or historical figure
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
